metadata.description: A physical L2/L3 network switch as a managed ASSET — the network-fabric peer of Compute.BareMetalHost. Its ports are Hardware.NetworkInterface components contained_by it (the same type serves host NICs and switch ports; §27 Identity distinguishes instances), with the §26 rollup on `ports`. Host NIC ↔ switch port physical links are the NetworkInterface `connected_to` adjacency (LLDP-discovered), which is what makes the estate graph traversable host → NIC → port → switch → power. Can exist unallocated (lifecycle_state: available, §28) and enter as Discovered via LLDP (brownfield ingest-then-adopt). Vendor specifics (UniFi, Catalyst, …) are the provider/extension, never the type.

lifecycle_state.description: Allocation state (common-elements §6, §28). available = inventoried/unallocated; allocated = adopted into the managed lifecycle.

composition_visibility.description: Whether ports are modeled as first-class Hardware.NetworkInterface entities (§26). opaque = the `ports` rollup only; transparent/selective = ports addressable individually (required for connected_to link records).

chassis_id.description: LLDP chassis identifier — normally the chassis MAC (IEEE 802.1AB Chassis ID TLV). Primary discovered-identity key; stable across renames/re-IPs.

system_name.description: Configured system name (LLDP System Name TLV) — mutable, secondary to chassis_id.

ports.description: Reconciled port rollup (§26) — aggregate of contained Hardware.NetworkInterface when modeled.

management_vlan.description: 802.1Q VLAN the management interface lives on, if tagged.

firmware_version.description: Observed firmware/OS version (LLDP System Description carries it) — Discovered-state data; drift input for fleet-consistency checks.

relationships.description: Power source(s) the switch draws from; a UPS-backed switch outlives the hosts in a reverse-topo shutdown (connectivity goes last).

adopts.role: discovered chassis identity (Chassis ID / System Name / System Description TLVs) — how a switch enters as a Discovered resource
topology vocabulary — a switch is a node whose termination points (ports) anchor links
